description: Letter from Yeḥezqel b. Eli ha-Kohen b. Yeḥezqel, in Jerusalem, to Eli ha-Kohen b. Ḥayyim (aka Abū l-Ḥasan ʿAllūn b. Yaʿīsh), in Fustat. In Judaeo-Arabic with the address in Arabic. Dating: second half of the 11th century. See also T-S Misc.28.171. The writer expresses his relief at the news that the addressee recovered from his illness. He devotes much space to excusing himself for failing to send any letters until now. He asks the addressee to remind 'the Rayyis' (probably identical with al-Rayyis Abū l-Ṭayyib in T-S Misc.28.171) to remind his son to fulfill the money order (suftaja) that had been sent. In a postscript, he conveys the news that the sister of Hiba ('who is with Shimʿon al-Rav') died, who was also the mother-in-law of Abūn b. Ṣedaqa. Information from Gil. ASE. NB: The first Goitein note card (#6240) belongs with DK 228.4 (PGPID 31342).
tags: illness letter 969-1517, Arabic address, suftaja, Illness, illness: marad, illness: mention
description_he: מכתב מיחזקאל ב' עלי הכהן ב' יחזקאל, ירושלים, לעלי הכהן ב' חיים (הידוע כאבו אל-חסן עלון ב' יעיש), פוסטאט. ביהודית-ערבית. המחצית השנייה של המאה ה-11. מביע הקלה על החלמת הנמען. מבקש מ'הראייס' להזכיר לבנו לקיים את הסופטג'ה.
